title: Documentation, Demonstration, Independent Defense, and Reflective Transfer · Claim Audit: Audience Documentation
summary: A polished final presentation shows the product but hides failed hypotheses, human evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, contribution limits, and what remains unproven. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone audience documentation increment in public capstone dossier and independent defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
Plan export, migration, retention, deletion, dependency shutdown, credential revocation, domain and client behavior, communication, and archive evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Audience Documentation · Theory Audience Documentation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A polished final story hides failed hypotheses, assistance, affected-user evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, and remaining non-claims. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Audience Documentation contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Audience Documentation · Theory Audience Documentation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail. For Predict Audience Documentation · Theory Audience Documentation, keep Audience Documentation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A polished final story hides failed hypotheses, assistance, affected-user evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, and remaining non-claims. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Audience Documentation condition decided your prediction.

content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

content.text: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Audience Documentation while producing public capstone dossier and independent defense.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A polished final story hides failed hypotheses, assistance, affected-user evidence, accessibility, threat decisions, incident recovery, and remaining non-claims. The worked example variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail. A defensible solution applies Audience Documentation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to public capstone dossier and independent defense.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-audience-documentation — Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
focus: capstone-audience-documentation
decision-evidence: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: One comprehensive README serves every audience well. This fails the Audience Documentation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: ACM IEEE AAAI Computer Science Curricula 2023 (CS2023 final report) — Controls competency-based progression, project work, professional practice, ethics, accessibility, security, testing, and the integration of knowledge with observable performance.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Provide affected-user, operator, maintainer, contributor, reviewer, security, accessibility, and decision-maker documentation at appropriate detail.
